intended_use: Lung AI software device is a Computer-Aided Detection (CADe) tool designed to assist in the detection of consolidation/atelectasis and pleural effusion during the review of lung ultrasound scans. The
intended_use_full: Lung AI software device is a Computer-Aided Detection (CADe) tool designed to assist in the detection of consolidation/atelectasis and pleural effusion during the review of lung ultrasound scans. The software is an adjunctive tool to alert users to the presence of regions of interest (ROI) with consolidation/atelectasis and pleural effusion within the analyzed lung ultrasound cine clip. Lung AI is intended to be used on images collected from the PLAPS point, in accordance with the BLUE protocol.
target_condition: Consolidation/atelectasis and pleural effusion in lung ultrasound
ground_truth: Two US board-certified experts experienced in point-of-care ultrasound and diagnostic radiology independently assessed presence/absence of findings per cine clip, with adjudication by a third expert in case of disagreement
other_metrics: Localization sensitivity: 85% (pleural effusion), 86% (consolidation/atelectasis); Localization specificity: 91% (pleural effusion), 94% (consolidation/atelectasis); MRMC AUC improvement: 0.035 for pleural effusion, 0.028 for consolidation; MRMC sensitivity improvement: 0.18 for pleural effusion, 0.16 for consolidation
